results.possibleIdentity: Scotty Snyder (born 1912) was an American painter and performance artist who was a prominent figure in the New York avant-garde scene during the late 1960s and 1970s. Based in Summit, New Jersey, she was associated with the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization and participated in their exhibition held from November 1968 to February 1969 (likely the landmark 'Some More Beginnings' show at the Brooklyn Museum). Snyder was a frequent collaborator with theater director Robert Wilson as a member of his Byrd Hoffman School of Byrds, appearing in major productions such as 'The King of Spain' (1969), 'The Life and Times of Sigmund Freud' (1969), and 'Deafman Glance' (1970). In 1981, she starred in the film 'Strong Medicine', written and directed by Richard Foreman. As a painter, her work was featured in solo and group exhibitions at the Summit Art Center (now the Visual Arts Center of New Jersey) in 1962 and 1972, and she was selected for the 1969 New England Exhibition of Painting and Sculpture at the Silvermine Guild of Artists. Performance artist Stuart Sherman honored her with a 1987 video portrait titled 'Scotty Snyder (All Around the Table)'.
